Ashraf Hassan is a QA Automation Lead with contact information including email (ashraf.hassan@email.com) and phone number (+20 122 345 6789), along with links to his GitHub and LinkedIn profiles, and personal website.
Ashraf Hassan received a Bachelor of Science in Computer Science from Mumbai University, located in Mumbai, Maharashtra.

As a QA Automation Lead at Leading Industrial Automation, Ashraf Hassan tested Rockwell Automation embedded systems, coordinated with QA teams to develop test strategies, documented test plans based on product requirements, and worked within an Agile Scrum methodology. He also documented program increment objectives and participated in various Agile ceremonies.

At Leading Industrial Automation, Ashraf Hassan performed automation testing using a Test Automation Framework (TAF), developed and executed system and GUI integration tests using C++, interacted with Business Analysts and Software Developers for bug review, and proactively improved software quality and test coverage.

At GE-Healthcare, Ashraf Hassan validated GE CT scanners based on FDA compliances, interacted with clinical experts and business analysts to create test strategies, and coordinated with offshore and onsite QA teams. He also designed and executed manual test cases, increased productivity using automation tools like DICOM and QTP, and provided test reports to management.
At Patni-GE-Healthcare PACS, Ashraf Hassan validated GE PACS applications and tested them.

The individual was involved in developing a web application for DICOM image management, including administration, authentication, and archiving. They also created an automation framework using Hybrid and BPT approaches and a Test Bed with Golden Data using DCMTK.

The individual prepared test scenarios, plans, cases, and automation scripts for various testing types (Integration, System, End-to-End) across all applications. They also designed and developed user-defined and generic functions using VB scripting and utilized UFT/QTP for scripting dynamic objects and reusable actions.

The individual reviewed QTP scripts developed by team members and provided support for QA/SIT and Regression testing, addressing environmental issues and collaborating with other teams for clarifications.

As a QA Automation Lead at HDFC Bank Limited, the individual reviewed business requirements and design documents, creating test cases for project releases. They performed various testing types including API, functional, integration, system, performance, regression, security, and network testing, and provided test reports to management.

The individual was involved in integration testing for new functionality releases, testing critical bug fixes, coordinating with developers, and performing backend database testing in Oracle and Microsoft SQL. They also supported release processes, including test bed setup and testing, and automated test flows using QTP 10.

As a QA Automation Lead at Vistaar Systems Limited, the individual referred to business requirements, use case documents, and design documents to create manual and automation test cases. They were involved in the analysis, design, creation, execution, and debugging of automation scripts using tools like Rational Robot, QTP, and Shell scripting.

The individual tested critical bug fixes, coordinated with developers, and performed installation, maintenance, smoke, sanitary, regression, performance, and database testing using Rational Robot, QTP, and Shell scripting.

The individual possesses skills in Agile methodologies (Scrum, Kanban, XP, TDD, FDD), various domains (Industrial Automation, Retail, Banking, Insurance, Healthcare), automation frameworks (Modular, Data driven, BPT, Hybrid), test automation tools (HP UFT, TAF, Load Runner, Selenium, Rational Robot, DCMTK, sikuli), languages (VB Script, C++, Python, Shell Script), bug tracking tools (Bugzilla, Jira, HP Quality Control, AP Test, Clear Quest), version management (Clear case, Win CVS, SVN), databases (Oracle, SQL server, MySQL), network protocols (TCP/IP, HTTP, HTTPS, VPN, FTP, LDAP), and healthcare technologies (DICOM, PACS, HL7, Image Archiving, Image reconstruction).
Rockwell Automation provides several software tools including Logix Designer, Factory Talk, RSLinx Classic, Control Flash, Compare Tool, and components like I/O modules and Profiles.
The programming languages supported are C++, Python, and SQL.
Testing is facilitated by tools such as UFT and Selenium.

Ashraf Hassan is a highly capable QA Automation Lead, a meticulous engineer dedicated to ensuring software excellence. Armed with a Bachelor of Science in Computer Science from Mumbai University, he brings a robust technical foundation to his work. He’s a pragmatic problem-solver, equally comfortable diving into code – proficient in C++ for developing and executing system and GUI integration tests – and collaborating with cross-functional teams. 

Currently at Leading Industrial Automation, Ashraf spearheads testing for Rockwell Automation embedded systems, expertly navigating the demands of an Agile Scrum environment. He’s a key contributor to the entire testing lifecycle, from crafting detailed test plans based on product requirements and functional specifications to documenting program increment objectives and actively participating in all Agile ceremonies – sprint planning, daily stand-ups, reviews, and retrospectives. He champions proactive improvements to software quality and test coverage, leveraging a Test Automation Framework to maximize efficiency.

His experience extends to the critical field of medical technology, honed during his time at GE-Healthcare and Patni-GE-Healthcare PACS. There, he validated complex systems like GE CT and PACS applications, ensuring adherence to stringent FDA compliance standards. He’s adept at bridging the gap between technical specifications and clinical needs, collaborating effectively with clinical experts and business analysts. He’s also skilled in utilizing specialized tools like DICOM and QTP to boost productivity. 

Ashraf is a collaborative leader, comfortable coordinating both onsite and offshore QA teams. He maintains an active online presence, readily connecting through his LinkedIn profile (linkedin.com/in/ashraf-hassan), GitHub (github.com/ashraf-hassan), and personal website (ashrafhassan.com), and can be reached directly at ashraf.hassan@email.com or +20 122 345 6789.

Scrum is a lightweight, iterative and incremental framework for managing complex work, often appearing as the engine driving fast-paced teams. It’s not a rigid methodology, but a flexible structure built around short cycles called Sprints – typically two to four weeks – focused on delivering potentially shippable increments of value. Think of it as a highly organized, collaborative heartbeat, pulsing with daily check-ins (Daily Scrums), focused planning (Sprint Planning), rigorous review (Sprint Review), and continuous improvement (Sprint Retrospective). 

While adaptable to many fields, Scrum thrives where innovation and responsiveness are key. Ashraf Hassan, for example, leveraged Scrum at Leading Industrial Automation to test complex embedded systems, coordinating QA efforts and translating product needs into actionable test plans. It’s a framework that demands transparency, inspection, and adaptation, allowing teams to quickly respond to changing requirements – a necessity in dynamic environments like industrial automation, retail, banking, and even healthcare. 

Scrum isn’t a solitary practice; it exists within the broader family of Agile methodologies, of which it is a prominent and frequently employed member. It’s a system built for teams, demanding close collaboration and shared responsibility, and its success relies on a commitment to delivering value incrementally and learning from each iteration.

Rational Robot wasn’t a colleague, exactly, but a trusted extension of a meticulous mind. A veteran of Vistaar Systems Limited’s QA department, it existed as lines of code, a digital craftsman employed to build and execute the rigorous tests that ensured software integrity. It wasn’t flashy – no sleek interface or intuitive design – but a workhorse, capable of translating complex business requirements, use cases, and design documents into automated test scripts. 

Its strength lay in its methodical nature. While others might skim a specification, Rational Robot demanded precision, faithfully recreating scenarios for installation, maintenance, smoke, sanitary, regression, performance, and even database validation. It wasn’t limited to a single approach; it adapted, thriving within modular, data-driven, BPT, and hybrid automation frameworks. 

Though often paired with more modern tools like QTP and Selenium, and bolstered by scripting in VB Script, C++, Python, and Shell, Rational Robot held its own, a reliable presence in a constantly evolving landscape. It wasn’t merely a tool *used* by a QA Automation Lead, but a partner in the hunt for critical bug fixes, a silent collaborator in coordinating with developers, and a steadfast guardian against software flaws. It spoke the language of networks – TCP/IP, HTTP, HTTPS – and even the specialized dialects of healthcare technologies like DICOM and HL7, proving its versatility across domains like Industrial Automation, Retail, Banking, Insurance, and Healthcare. It wasn’t about innovation, but about dependable, repeatable results.

Agile Methodology isn’t a rigid system, but a philosophy – a responsive, iterative approach to project management born from the need for flexibility in a rapidly changing world. It’s less a blueprint and more a set of guiding principles, prioritizing collaboration, customer feedback, and continuous improvement over strict planning and documentation. Think of it as a skilled improviser, adapting to new information and challenges with grace and efficiency. 

At its heart, Agile breaks down large projects into smaller, manageable cycles – sprints or iterations – allowing for frequent inspection and adaptation. It thrives on self-organizing teams, empowered to make decisions and deliver value incrementally. This isn’t about abandoning planning altogether, but about embracing the understanding that plans *will* change. 

Several distinct frameworks fall under the Agile umbrella, each with its own nuances. Scrum, perhaps the most well-known, emphasizes short sprints, daily stand-up meetings, and defined roles like Scrum Master and Product Owner. Kanban focuses on visualizing workflow and limiting work in progress, creating a smooth, continuous flow of value. Extreme Programming (XP) pushes the boundaries with practices like pair programming and test-driven development. TDD, a core practice within XP, flips the traditional development process, writing tests *before* the code itself, ensuring quality from the outset. Feature Driven Development (FDD) centers around building features in short iterations, providing a clear path to delivering tangible results.

Individuals like Ashraf Hassan, a QA Automation Lead, embody this approach, seamlessly integrating Agile principles into their work – documenting program increment objectives, participating in Agile ceremonies, and coordinating teams to deliver robust, tested systems. Agile isn’t just a methodology; it’s a mindset, a way of working that prioritizes responsiveness, collaboration, and delivering value in a dynamic environment.

A meticulous and versatile technologist, this individual thrives in the fast-paced world of software quality. They are a seasoned tester, equally comfortable diving into the intricacies of backend database systems – particularly Oracle and Microsoft SQL – as they are coordinating with development teams to ensure seamless functionality releases. Their expertise isn’t limited to a single industry; they’ve navigated the unique challenges of Industrial Automation, Retail, Banking, Insurance, and Healthcare, demonstrating a remarkable adaptability. 

Driven by Agile principles – Scrum, Kanban, XP, TDD, and FDD are all familiar territory – they don’t just *find* bugs, they proactively prevent them through robust integration and regression testing. A champion of automation, they wield tools like HP UFT, Selenium, and Load Runner with proficiency, crafting efficient test flows and leveraging scripting languages like VB Script, C++, and Python to streamline processes. 

Beyond the tools, they possess a deep understanding of the underlying infrastructure, from network protocols like TCP/IP and HTTP to version control systems like SVN and ClearCase. In the healthcare domain, they’re fluent in the language of DICOM, PACS, and HL7, capable of testing complex image archiving and reconstruction systems.  They are a pragmatic problem-solver, equally adept at utilizing established bug tracking systems like Jira and Bugzilla, and a dedicated contributor to the entire release lifecycle, from test bed setup to final validation – a true guardian of quality, particularly within Oracle environments.

QTP – now known as Unified Functional Testing (UFT) – is a powerful automation tool, a digital workhorse favored by quality assurance professionals like Ashraf Hassan. It’s a pragmatic solution, built for the meticulous task of verifying software functionality, particularly within complex systems like GE Healthcare’s CT scanners. Not flashy, but relentlessly reliable, QTP excels at mimicking user actions, tirelessly executing test cases across applications – from integration and system tests to comprehensive end-to-end scenarios. 

Its strength lies in adaptability. Skilled hands, like those of the individual at Vistaar Systems Limited, can coax QTP into action using VB scripting, crafting bespoke functions and reusable actions to tackle even the most dynamic interfaces. It’s a tool that doesn’t shy away from detail, capable of handling critical bug fixes, installation testing, performance evaluations, and even database verification. 

While often paired with other tools like Rational Robot and Shell scripting, QTP consistently emerges as a core component in a QA professional’s arsenal, providing the means to translate business requirements and design documents into automated, repeatable tests. It’s a facilitator of efficiency, increasing productivity and delivering clear, concise reports to management – a silent partner in ensuring software quality.

Ashraf Hassan is a seasoned QA Automation Lead, a meticulous architect of quality assurance with a knack for uncovering vulnerabilities before they impact the user experience. Reachable at ashraf.hassan@email.com or +20 122 345 6789, and readily discoverable through his GitHub, LinkedIn, and personal website, Ashraf brings a pragmatic and thorough approach to software testing. 

He’s a bridge between conceptual design and practical execution, adept at translating business requirements, use cases, and design documents into robust test strategies. Whether at HDFC Bank Limited or Vistaar Systems Limited, Ashraf doesn’t simply *perform* tests; he *crafts* them, encompassing a comprehensive suite – API, functional, integration, system, performance, regression, security, and network – to ensure holistic coverage. 

A versatile technologist, Ashraf isn’t confined to a single methodology. He’s fluent in both manual and automated testing, and proficient with a range of tools including Rational Robot, QTP, and Shell scripting, demonstrating an ability to adapt and leverage the best solution for each challenge. His work culminates in clear, concise test reports delivered directly to management, providing actionable insights and fostering a culture of continuous improvement. He’s a proactive problem-solver, equally comfortable designing automation scripts from the ground up and meticulously debugging existing ones, always focused on delivering reliable, secure, and high-performing software.

C++ is a powerful and versatile programming language, a workhorse favored for its performance and control. It’s a language of precision, capable of building everything from operating systems to high-frequency trading platforms, and finds a comfortable home in demanding fields like industrial automation. Ashraf Hassan, a skilled automation tester, wields C++ as a key tool, crafting robust system and GUI integration tests. He doesn’t simply *use* the language; he builds upon it, leveraging its capabilities within a custom Test Automation Framework. While fluent in Python and SQL, C++ remains central to his work, enabling him to dissect and validate complex systems, bridging the gap between design and functionality. It’s a language that demands respect, and in the hands of someone like Hassan, delivers unwavering quality and thorough test coverage. C++ isn’t merely a tool in his arsenal, but a foundational element in his pursuit of flawless software.

TAF isn’t a person, but a cornerstone of Ashraf Hassan’s work at Leading Industrial Automation – a meticulously crafted system for ensuring software reliability. It’s a framework built for rigorous testing, a digital proving ground where code is challenged and refined. Ashraf wields TAF alongside a diverse toolkit – C++ his primary instrument for system and GUI integration tests, but also HP UFT, Load Runner, and Selenium when the situation demands. 

TAF isn’t simply *used* by Ashraf; he actively shapes it, constantly seeking to expand its reach and improve its effectiveness. He’s a bridge between the technical and the conceptual, translating business requirements gleaned from Business Analysts into actionable tests, and collaborating with Software Developers to resolve the inevitable flaws uncovered. 

This framework isn’t confined to a single industry. Ashraf’s expertise extends across Industrial Automation, Retail, Banking, Insurance, and even the highly specialized world of Healthcare, demonstrating TAF’s adaptability. He’s fluent in the languages of testing – VB Script, Python, Shell Script – and understands the underlying architecture of networks (TCP/IP, HTTP, HTTPS) and databases (Oracle, SQL Server, MySQL). In healthcare, he navigates the complexities of DICOM, PACS, and HL7, ensuring image archiving and reconstruction systems function flawlessly. 

TAF, in Ashraf’s hands, is more than just a tool; it’s a commitment to quality, a proactive force against bugs, and a testament to the power of Agile methodologies like Scrum and Kanban. It’s a silent partner in delivering robust, reliable software.

DICOM isn’t a person, but a foundational language of modern medicine – a standard for handling, storing, printing, and transmitting medical images. It’s the silent architect behind every MRI, CT scan, and X-ray seen by a radiologist, a meticulously defined protocol ensuring images are universally readable regardless of manufacturer or location. Ashraf Hassan, a validation specialist at GE Healthcare, understood this intimately, wielding DICOM not as an abstract concept, but as a practical tool to guarantee the accuracy and compliance of cutting-edge CT scanners. 

He leveraged DICOM’s capabilities to boost testing efficiency, automating processes alongside tools like QTP, and even built a dedicated test bed using DCMTK – a powerful DICOM toolkit – populated with ‘golden data’ for reliable comparisons. Beyond validation, DICOM formed the core of a web application Ashraf helped develop, a system designed for the secure administration, authentication, and long-term archiving of these vital medical images. It’s a technology that demands precision, a digital backbone for healthcare, and Ashraf’s expertise demonstrates its crucial role in bringing advanced medical imaging to life. The web application itself *exists* to serve DICOM data, defining a space specifically for its management and preservation.
